message:
feat(fuse): wire FS_IOC_GETFSSYSFSPATH ioctl (bd-04xv6)

Implements the Linux 6.7+ FS_IOC_GETFSSYSFSPATH ioctl through the
FUSE dispatcher. The ioctl is encoded as
_IOR(0x15, 1, struct fs_sysfs_path) on x86_64 and returns the 129-byte
struct {
    u8 len;
    u8 name[128];
}
that records the kernel-side sysfs path of the underlying block
device (e.g. /sys/block/sdaN). Userspace probes — systemd-mount,
blkid, util-linux — call this to discover the device's sysfs entry
and silently skip when len == 0, treating empty as "no sysfs
visibility" rather than an error. tmpfs and overlayfs follow the
same convention.

ffs-core (FsOps trait):

* New fs_sysfs_path() method on FsOps with a default impl returning
  Vec::new(). FUSE backends backed by ByteDevice have no /sys entry
  to surface, so empty-path is the correct production behavior for
  both ext4 and btrfs OpenFs adapters today. Backends with sysfs
  visibility (a future loop-device adapter, for example) override
  the default and return up to 128 bytes; longer paths are rejected
  as EINVAL by the dispatcher.

ffs-fuse:

* New constants FS_IOC_GETFSSYSFSPATH (0x8081_1501),
  FS_IOC_GETFSSYSFSPATH_SIZE (129) and
  FS_IOC_GETFSSYSFSPATH_NAME_MAX (128) pinning the wire format.
* New helper FrankenFuse::encode_fs_sysfs_path_response that builds
  the 1-byte len + 128-byte zero-padded name layout. Returns EINVAL
  for paths longer than 128 bytes; debug_asserts the encoded buffer
  length to catch padding regressions.
* Dispatcher case in handle_ioctl routes FS_IOC_GETFSSYSFSPATH to
  ops.fs_sysfs_path(), enforces out_size >= 129, and surfaces both
  encoder errors and underlying FsOps errors through IoctlResult.

Tests cover the empty-path (production) encoding, a non-empty
fixture round-trip, the short-buffer EINVAL path, the 128-byte
maximum-length path, and the over-long-path rejection — five total
new tests in the ffs-fuse mod tests module, plus an IoctlCall fixture
addition (FsSysfsPath) so the dispatcher fixture can record the call
shape.
